metadata.notes: Golden index and candidate index are 1-based. null means no match.

deviations.pr_title: Async import of the appStore packages

true_positives.golden_comment: The code uses forEach with async callbacks, which causes asynchronous operations (e.g., calendar/video event deletions, payment refunds) to run concurrently without being awaited. This 'fire-and-forget' behavior leads to unhandled promise rejections, race conditions, and incomplete cleanup, as surrounding try-catch blocks cannot properly handle errors from these unawaited promises. Replace forEach with for...of loops or Promise.all() with map() to ensure proper sequential execution and error handling.
true_positives.finding_description: forEach-async-discard in vital/reschedule.ts

false_positives.finding_description: forEach-async-discard in wipemycalother/reschedule.ts
forEach-async-discard in bookings.tsx
Silent type drift — getCalendarCredentials stores Promise without await
Composition opacity — return type unchanged despite Promise property
Eager parallel loading — import() fires at eval time, not on access
Dual key derivation — dirName vs type.split("_").join("")

false_negatives.golden_comment: Consider adding try-catch around the await to handle import failures gracefully

deviations.pr_title: feat: 2fa backup codes

true_positives.golden_comment: Error message mentions 'backup code login' but this is a disable endpoint, not login
true_positives.finding_description: Exhausted codes return wrong error (disable)

true_positives.golden_comment: Because backupCodes are decrypted and mutated in memory before being written back, two concurrent login requests using the same backupCode could both pass this check and update, so a single backup code may effectively be accepted more than once if used concurrently, weakening the intended one-time-use semantics.
true_positives.finding_description: TOCTOU race on backup code invalidation

false_positives.finding_description: Missing encryption key guard in setup.ts
Exhausted codes return wrong error (next-auth)
Blob URL not revoked on modal close
Non-enforcing 2FA switch assertion
No e2e tests for backup code auth paths
Backup code length as bare literals
Duplicated verification logic
Clipboard write not awaited
Missing .trim() on backup code input
Cancel doesn't reset twoFactorLostAccess
Escape bypasses resetState in enable modal

false_negatives.golden_comment: The exported function TwoFactor handles backup codes and is in BackupCode.tsx. Inconsistent naming.

Backup code validation is case-sensitive due to the use of indexOf(). This causes validation to fail if a user enters uppercase hex characters, as backup codes should be case-insensitive for a better user experience.

deviations.pr_title: fix: handle collective multiple host on destinationCalendar

true_positives.golden_comment: Potential null reference if mainHostDestinationCalendar is undefined if evt.destinationCalendar is null or an empty array
true_positives.finding_description: EventManager null dereference on mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration

true_positives.golden_comment: Logic error: when externalCalendarId is provided, you're searching for a calendar where externalId === externalCalendarId, but this will always fail since you're looking for a calendar that matches itself. Should likely find by credentialId or use different logic.
true_positives.finding_description: Google Calendar updateEvent tautological .find() — falsy externalCalendarId used as search value

true_positives.golden_comment: Logic inversion in organization creation: The slug property is now conditionally set when IS_TEAM_BILLING_ENABLED is true, instead of when it's false as originally intended. This change, combined with requestedSlug still being set when IS_TEAM_BILLING_ENABLED is true, results in both properties being set when billing is enabled, and neither when disabled
true_positives.finding_description: Organization create handler inverted slug/requestedSlug logic

false_positives.finding_description: Google Calendar deleteEvent tautological .find() — same pattern as F-01
loadUsers removes organization select from Prisma query
evt.destinationCalendar?.push silently no-ops when base is null
updateAllCalendarEvents missing credential guard before updateEvent
console.error removed from updateAllCalendarEvents error handler
Duplicated credential-fetch-from-DB logic across two methods
Asymmetric externalId propagation in createAllCalendarEvents
requestReschedule missing user destinationCalendar fallback
Dropped video credentialId in result mapping
Booking DB write connects only destinationCalendar[0]

false_negatives.golden_comment: The optional chaining on mainHostDestinationCalendar?.integration is redundant since you already check mainHostDestinationCalendar in the ternary condition.

The Calendar interface now requires createEvent(event, credentialId), but some implementations (e.g., Lark/Office365) still declare createEvent(event) only—this breaks the interface contract (also applies to other locations in the PR).

deviations.pr_title: feat: convert InsightsBookingService to use Prisma.sql raw queries

true_positives.golden_comment: In getBaseConditions(), the else if (filterConditions) and final else branches are unreachable. This is because getAuthorizationConditions() always returns a non-null Prisma.Sql object, making authConditions always truthy, which means only the first two if/else if conditions are ever evaluated.
true_positives.finding_description: Dead conditional guards in `getBaseConditions`

false_positives.finding_description: Caching tests removed while caching logic remains

false_negatives.golden_comment: Fetching userIdsFromOrg only when teamsFromOrg.length > 0 can exclude org-level members for orgs without child teams; consider deriving from teamIds (which includes orgId) or removing the guard so org-only orgs still include member user bookings.

deviations.pr_title: Comprehensive workflow reminder management for booking lifecycle events

true_positives.golden_comment: Asynchronous functions deleteScheduledEmailReminder and deleteScheduledSMSReminder are called without await inside forEach loops. This occurs during booking rescheduling/cancellation, and workflow/workflow step deletion/updates. Consequently, scheduled workflow reminders may not be reliably cancelled, potentially leaving them active.
true_positives.finding_description: `forEach(async ...)` fire-and-forget in `workflows.tsx`

true_positives.golden_comment: When immediateDelete is true, the deleteScheduledEmailReminder function cancels the SendGrid email but fails to delete the corresponding WorkflowReminder record from the database. This creates orphaned database entries and is inconsistent with the immediateDelete: false path, which marks the record as cancelled. The SendGrid DELETE API call is also omitted in this path.
true_positives.finding_description: `immediateDelete` path missing DB cleanup — orphans WorkflowReminder records

false_positives.finding_description: Cron partial failure leaves stranded DB records (SendGrid cancelled but not deleted)
Inconsistent `immediateDelete` between two rescheduling paths
Missing SendGrid DELETE call in `immediateDelete` branch (regression)
No lower bound on `scheduledDate` in cron cancellation query
Cron cancel path also missing SendGrid DELETE
Cron doesn't guard against null `referenceId` before SendGrid call
Fire-and-forget in `handleNewBooking.ts` rescheduling path
Fire-and-forget regression in `bookings.tsx` — prior `await Promise.all` eliminated
Fire-and-forget in `handleCancelBooking.ts` — cancellations removed from `prismaPromises`

deviations.pr_title: Advanced date override handling and timezone compatibility improvements

true_positives.golden_comment: Incorrect end time calculation using slotStartTime instead of slotEndTime
true_positives.finding_description: Copy-paste error — `end` computed from `slotStartTime`

true_positives.golden_comment: Using === for dayjs object comparison will always return false as it compares object references, not values. Use .isSame() method instead: dayjs(date.start).add(utcOffset, 'minutes').isSame(dayjs(date.end).add(utcOffset, minutes))
true_positives.finding_description: Dead conditional guard — dayjs object identity `===`

false_positives.finding_description: UTC offset sign inversion in date override day-matching
Asymmetric interval boundary in date override check
Inverted find() predicate in working-hours check
UTC weekday vs organizer-local weekday mismatch
Divergent organizerTimeZone between getSlots and checkIfIsAvailable
Missing undefined guard on optional override.timeZone
Bare string literals in test assertions
String-keyed timezone lookup in test
Date override branch bypasses busy-time check
Test does not cover booking conflicts within date override windows
Multi-override short-circuit via find()
Spread ordering allows override.timeZone overwrite
dateOverrides and workingHours never wired to checkIfIsAvailable
DST boundary in offset calculation

deviations.pr_title: OAuth credential sync and app integration enhancements

true_positives.golden_comment: The parseRefreshTokenResponse function incorrectly sets refresh_token to the hardcoded string 'refresh_token' when it's missing from the OAuth refresh token response. This invalidates the token, breaking subsequent token refreshes and causing authentication failures.
true_positives.finding_description: Sentinel refresh token persisted to credential store

true_positives.golden_comment: Invalid Zod schema syntax. Computed property keys like [z.string().toString()] are not valid in Zod object schemas and will cause runtime errors.
true_positives.finding_description: Ineffective minimumTokenResponseSchema due to key collision

true_positives.golden_comment: parseRefreshTokenResponse returns a Zod safeParse result ({ success, data, error }), not the credential key object. Persisting that as key stores the wrapper instead of the token payload; we should store the parsed data or use schema parse.
true_positives.finding_description: Google Calendar stores SafeParseReturnType wrapper instead of token data

true_positives.golden_comment: When APP_CREDENTIAL_SHARING_ENABLED and CALCOM_CREDENTIAL_SYNC_ENDPOINT are set, the refreshFunction helper returns the fetch Response, but several callers (for example GoogleCalendarService.refreshAccessToken expecting res.data, and HubspotCalendarService.refreshAccessToken expecting a HubspotToken) assume it returns the integration-specific token object. That mismatch will cause runtime errors in the sync-enabled path unless the return type or those call sites are adjusted.
true_positives.finding_description: refreshOAuthTokens returns incompatible types across paths

true_positives.golden_comment: When the sync endpoint path is used, res is a fetch Response and has no .data; res?.data will be undefined and token.access_token will throw at runtime. This relies on a consistent return shape from refreshOAuthTokens, which isn’t guaranteed currently.
true_positives.finding_description: refreshOAuthTokens returns incompatible types across paths

false_positives.finding_description: Dead conditional guard in Salesforce caller
Office365 diagnostic logging removed
Timing-unsafe webhook secret comparison
Zoho Bigin passes credentialId instead of userId
AES encryption key length not validated
Webhook endpoint accepts all HTTP methods
Unhandled ZodError on malformed webhook body
Office365 dead guard in spread expression
Salesforce unconditional token refresh at construction

deviations.pr_title: SMS workflow reminder retry count tracking

true_positives.golden_comment: Using retryCount: reminder.retryCount + 1 reads a possibly stale value and can lose increments under concurrency; consider an atomic increment via Prisma (increment: 1) to avoid race conditions (also applies to the similar update in the catch block).
true_positives.finding_description: Non-atomic increment allows duplicate SMS and lost counts

true_positives.golden_comment: The deletion logic in scheduleSMSReminders.ts incorrectly deletes non-SMS workflow reminders (e.g., Email, WhatsApp) that have retryCount > 1. This occurs because the retryCount condition within the OR clause for deletion lacks a method: WorkflowMethods.SMS filter, causing it to apply to all reminder types instead of only SMS reminders, which is the intended scope of this function.
true_positives.finding_description: Missing method filter on retry-based deletion OR arm

false_positives.finding_description: DB write in catch can swallow original error
Verbatim duplication of retryCount increment
Comment describes only one of two deletion conditions
Fetch has no retryCount guard; relies on prior delete
Missing index on retryCount for recurring bulk delete

deviations.pr_title: Add guest management functionality to existing bookings

true_positives.golden_comment: Case sensitivity bypass in email blacklist
true_positives.finding_description: Case-insensitive blacklist bypass

true_positives.golden_comment: The logic for checking team admin/owner permissions is incorrect. This condition uses AND (&&) which requires both isTeamAdmin AND isTeamOwner to be true, but it should use OR (||) since a user needs to be either an admin OR an owner to have permission.
true_positives.finding_description: Authorization AND vs OR logic inversion

true_positives.golden_comment: This calls the email sender with the original guests, so existing attendees included in the input will be treated as new when sending notifications, leading to incorrect emails.
true_positives.finding_description: Raw vs filtered guest list in email dispatch

true_positives.golden_comment: uniqueGuests filters out existing attendees and blacklisted emails but does not deduplicate duplicates within the input; createMany can insert duplicate attendee rows if the client submits repeated emails.
true_positives.finding_description: Case-sensitive attendee dedup allows duplicates

true_positives.golden_comment: Starting with an array containing an empty string may cause validation issues. Consider starting with an empty array [] and handling the empty state in the MultiEmail component instead.
true_positives.finding_description: Dead guard — length === 0 never triggers

false_positives.finding_description: Zero-value sentinel teamId ?? 0 in auth checks
Silent email error discard — err object unused
Zod schema recreated inside component per render
Untranslated tooltip "Remove email"
Index-based React key in dynamic list
Organizer duplicate notification
Dead error fallback — template literal always truthy
No booking status guard — guests added to cancelled/past bookings
Fragile attendees[0].name in organizer email subject
0 sentinel in organizer lookup
isInvalidEmail not cleared on input change
Broken label-input association (accessibility)
Server schema missing uniqueness validation
Case-sensitive email template dispatch

deviations.pr_title: feat: add calendar cache status and actions (#22532)

true_positives.golden_comment: The updateManyByCredentialId call uses an empty data object, which prevents Prisma's @updatedAt decorator from updating the updatedAt timestamp. This results in inaccurate cache status tracking, as the timestamp isn't updated when the cache is refreshed. To fix this, explicitly set the updatedAt field.
true_positives.finding_description: Empty data `{}` to updateMany may not touch SelectedCalendar.updatedAt

true_positives.golden_comment: logic: macOS-specific sed syntax with empty string after -i flag will fail on Linux systems
true_positives.finding_description: Shell script uses macOS-only `sed -i ''` syntax, fails on Linux

false_positives.finding_description: Migration comment contradicts actual SQL (comment-code drift)
deleteCache handler throws plain Error instead of TRPCError (500 for auth failure)
Hardcoded "en-US" locale in Intl.DateTimeFormat ignoring user locale
deleteCache handler bypasses CalendarCacheRepository
Cache deletion does not invalidate connectedCalendars query (stale UI)
Second render site silently gains disableConnectionModification guard
Delete-cache confirm button has no loading/disabled guard
Empty actions container rendered when component returns null

deviations.pr_title: FEATURE: automatically downsize large images

true_positives.golden_comment: The downsize method is defined twice. The second definition, which expects a single dimensions string parameter, overrides the first, which expected separate max_width and max_height parameters. This makes the original method unreachable and breaks existing code that calls it with separate width and height arguments.
true_positives.finding_description: Duplicate `downsize` definition shadows 4-arg form — ArgumentError for existing callers

true_positives.golden_comment: Hardcoding maxSizeKB = 10 * 1024 ignores Discourse.SiteSettings['max_' + type + '_size_kb'], so the client-side limit can diverge from server-side and per-type settings (also applies to the 413 handler below).
true_positives.finding_description: Client-side 10MB limit applies to all upload types, not just images

false_positives.finding_description: 10MB hardcoded as three independent bare literals
"80%" reduces pixel dimensions, not file size or quality
413 handler shows hardcoded 10MB, not actual web server limit
No post-loop guard — oversized file uploads proceed silently
Comment-code drift — comment overstates reduction guarantee
Asymmetric API — resize takes numeric args, downsize takes string
`optimize` signature changed without making it private
Zero-value sentinel silently disables auto-downsize
In-place ImageMagick convert with identical source/dest path

false_negatives.golden_comment: Passing 80% as the dimensions can fail for animated GIFs when allow_animated_thumbnails is true, since the animated path uses gifsicle --resize-fit which expects WxH geometry, not a percentage; downsizing would then silently fail.

deviations.pr_title: Add comprehensive email validation for blocked users

true_positives.golden_comment: BlockedEmail.should_block_email? method has side effects during a read operation - it updates statistics even when just checking if an email should be blocked. This could cause race conditions in concurrent environments and makes the method name misleading.
true_positives.finding_description: `should_block?` mixes query with stat-tracking write side effects

true_positives.golden_comment: Regex pattern @(#{domains}) only matches domain suffixes, not full domains. evil.example.com would match whitelist entry example.com.

false_positives.finding_description: Case-sensitive email matching allows trivial bypass of blocks

deviations.pr_title: Enhance embed URL handling and validation system

true_positives.golden_comment: SSRF vulnerability using open(url) without validation
true_positives.finding_description: SSRF via Kernel#open in import_remote

true_positives.golden_comment: The current origin validation using indexOf is insufficient and can be bypassed. An attacker could use a malicious domain like evil-discourseUrl.com to pass this check.
true_positives.finding_description: Origin check uses substring match instead of equality

true_positives.golden_comment: The TopicEmbed.import method is susceptible to a NoMethodError if the contents parameter is nil when attempting to append a string, and an XSS vulnerability due to unescaped url interpolation in the generated HTML.
true_positives.finding_description: XSS via unescaped URL in HTML attribute

false_positives.finding_description: Migration default sets all existing posts to raw_html
XSS via unescaped request.referer in inline JS
Stored XSS via unsanitized RSS feed content
Nil crash on i.content in PollFeed
Single-URL retrieval triggers full RSS feed import
Content mutation via << includes I18n footer in SHA1
No error handling for network failures
poll_feed method has zero test coverage
Bare port literals without scheme correlation
Protocol-relative URLs corrupted by absolutize_urls

false_negatives.golden_comment: postMessage targetOrigin should be the origin (scheme+host+port), not the full referrer URL; using the full URL will cause the message to be dropped and prevent resizing.

The code sets X-Frame-Options: ALLOWALL which completely disables clickjacking protection. The referer validation can be bypassed (referer headers are easily spoofed), and the fallback to empty string for nil referer masks validation failures.
The ERB block closes with end if, which is invalid Ruby/ERB and will raise at render; it should just be end to close the if block.

deviations.pr_title: Optimize header layout performance with flexbox mixins

true_positives.golden_comment: Mixing float: left with flexbox causes layout issues. Further this PR removes the float-based right alignment for .d-header .panel, which may cause the login panel in the non-Ember/noscript header (where .panel is nested inside .row and not a flex item) to stack under the title instead of remaining right-aligned.
true_positives.finding_description: `order` on `.extra-info-wrapper` with no flex parent; retained float on `.badge-wrapper`

true_positives.golden_comment: -ms-align-items never existed in any version of IE/Edge; the correct legacy property is -ms-flex-align.
true_positives.finding_description: Dead `-ms-align-items` vendor prefix in `align-items` mixin

false_positives.finding_description: Padding unit change from `em` to `%` on `.small-action-desc`
Latent `-moz-box-ordinal-group` offset mismatch in `order` mixin

deviations.pr_title: UX: show complete URL path if website domain is same as instance domain

true_positives.golden_comment: The include_website_name method is missing the required ? suffix. Rails serializers expect include_ methods to end with ? for conditional attribute inclusion, a convention followed by other methods in this serializer. Without it, the website_name attribute may not be conditionally included as intended. Additionally, the '.' << website_host string concatenation should be replaced with '.' + website_host or '.#{website_host}' to avoid mutating string literals, which can lead to issues.
true_positives.finding_description: `"." << website_host` mutates string literal; raises FrozenError with frozen_string_literal

false_positives.finding_description: `URI(website.to_s)` parsed up to 4 times redundantly in one method call
elsif branch (same-depth subdomain matching) has zero test coverage
No test for pathless URL or root-path-only URL in same-domain scenario
Bare `rescue nil` catches all StandardError subclasses, not just URI::InvalidURIError
Single-label host (bare TLD) passes `ends_with?` check, false-matching as parent domain

deviations.pr_title: scale-color $lightness must use $secondary for dark themes

true_positives.golden_comment: This change for desktop/user.css changes $primary from 30% to 50% for the light theme; most other changes preserve the original $primary value and move the complement to $secondary for dark. Consider reviewing this (also applies to a similar .name change in the mobile variant).
true_positives.finding_description: desktop/user.scss .name: both args set to 50% instead of 30%/70%

true_positives.golden_comment: In topic-post.css the original code used $lightness: 70% but the replacement uses $lightness: 30% for the light theme. This makes the text significantly darker than intended.
true_positives.finding_description: desktop/topic-post.scss: light/dark args swapped (30%↔70%)

false_positives.finding_description: mobile/modal.scss: light/dark args swapped (70%↔30%)

mobile/user.scss .name: both args set to 50% instead of 30%/70%
Two distinct error modes in 5 of ~85 transformations

false_negatives.golden_comment: In .topic-meta-data h5 a, the original code had color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%) but was changed to dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 30%)). The lightness for the light theme changed from 30% to 70%, which is a dramatic inversion

Fallback scope expanded from production-only to all environments including test

Consider normalizing the input locale (e.g., to a symbol) when checking/loading here to avoid double-loading if the same locale is passed as a String vs Symbol (also applies to other locations in the PR).

deviations.pr_title: FEATURE: Can edit category/host relationships for embedding

true_positives.golden_comment: NoMethodError before_validation in EmbeddableHost
true_positives.finding_description: before_validation nil crash on host

true_positives.golden_comment: The update and destroy methods in Admin::EmbeddableHostsController do not validate the existence of the EmbeddableHost record retrieved by ID. If EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first returns nil (i.e., the host does not exist), attempting to call methods on the nil object (e.g., save_host or destroy) will result in a NoMethodError.
true_positives.finding_description: Nil dereference in update/destroy for missing records

false_positives.finding_description: SQL injection in migration via string interpolation
EmbeddingController#update silently ignores all payload
Non-global underscore replace in basePath
Fabricator files have swapped content
Controller specs test only inheritance, no actions
"is false if embeddable_host is blank" passes vacuously
Missing explicit setup for "no host" test condition
[]` fallback and null entries in plural hydration
Missing categories sideload in EmbeddingSerializer
Missing .catch on destroyRecord in delete action
Missing strong parameters guard in save_host
Unguarded nil dereference on PG result in migration
Host validation regex rejects valid TLDs > 5 chars
Missing array guard before .map() in plural hydration

false_negatives.golden_comment: record_for_host compares lower(host) = ? but does not normalize the parameter’s case, so mixed‑case referer hosts may fail to match even though comparison intends to be case‑insensitive.

Because this migration inserts embeddable_hosts rows with raw SQL, any existing embeddable_hosts values that include http:// or /https:// or path segments won’t go through the EmbeddableHost model’s normalization, so the new host lookup (which compares only the bare host) may fail for migrated data. Consider ensuring that migrated hosts are normalized to the same format as newly created EmbeddableHost records so existing embedding configurations keep working.

deviations.pr_title: Anonymous: Add configurable device limit

true_positives.golden_comment: Race condition: Multiple concurrent requests could pass the device count check simultaneously and create devices beyond the limit. Consider using a database transaction or lock.
true_positives.finding_description: TOCTOU race: count-then-insert device limit check is not atomic

true_positives.golden_comment: Anonymous authentication now fails entirely if anonDeviceService.TagDevice returns ErrDeviceLimitReached. Previously, device tagging was asynchronous and non-blocking. This change prevents anonymous users from authenticating when the device limit is reached.
true_positives.finding_description: `ErrDeviceLimitReached` blocks anonymous auth entirely — fire-and-forget converted to sync auth gate

true_positives.golden_comment: Time window calculation inconsistency: Using device.UpdatedAt.UTC().Add(-anonymousDeviceExpiration) as the lower bound but device.UpdatedAt as the current time may not match the intended logic. Consider using time.Now().UTC() consistently.
true_positives.finding_description: `updateDevice` WHERE clause anchors time window on `device.UpdatedAt` instead of `time.Now()`

false_positives.finding_description: Zero-value sentinel ambiguity: Go int64 always sends `0`, TS type expects `undefined` for "no limit"
Duplicate `anonymousDeviceExpiration` constant in `database.go` and `api.go`
Debug-level log before hard auth failure — log severity doesn't match operational impact
Panic recovery removed when goroutine was replaced with synchronous call
Interface-to-concrete coupling: constructor no longer accepts `AnonStore` interface
Request context passed with no timeout — removed 2-minute timeout constant
`TagDevice` now propagates all `tagDeviceUI` errors, not just `ErrDeviceLimitReached`
Test covers rejection path only; update success path for existing device at limit is untested
Closure captures and mutates outer `args` variable — corrupts argument list on retry

false_negatives.golden_comment: This call won’t compile: dbSession.Exec(args...) is given a []interface{} where the first element is the query, but Exec’s signature requires a first parameter of type string (not an interface{} splat).
Returning ErrDeviceLimitReached when no rows were updated is misleading; the device might not exist.

deviations.pr_title: AuthZService: improve authz caching

true_positives.golden_comment: The test comment says the cached permissions 'allow access', but the map stores false for dashboards:uid:dash1, so checkPermission will still treat this scope as not allowed.
true_positives.finding_description: "Should deny on explicit cache deny entry" uses `false` in permCache — non-enforcing fixture

false_positives.finding_description: Denial cache key collision via underscore delimiter in name/parent fields
Missing `permissionCacheUsage` metric on checkPermission error path
Denial cache key uses raw UserUID, permission cache uses resolved UID
"Outdated cache" test exercises same code path as cache miss test

false_negatives.golden_comment: The Check operation exhibits asymmetric cache trust logic: cached permission grants are trusted and returned immediately, but cached denials from the same permission cache are ignored, leading to a fresh database lookup. This allows stale cached grants to provide access to revoked resources, posing a security risk.

deviations.pr_title: Plugins: Chore: Renamed instrumentation middleware to metrics middleware

true_positives.golden_comment: The traceID is no longer logged for plugin requests. During a refactoring, the tracing import and the logic to extract and add traceID from the context to log parameters were removed from the LoggerMiddleware. The newly introduced ContextualLoggerMiddleware does not add this information, resulting in missing traceID in plugin request logs and impacting debugging and request tracing capabilities.
true_positives.finding_description: traceID field silently removed from log output

false_positives.finding_description: TestLogger.FromContext returns zero-state logger, ignoring context
grafanaInfraLogWrapper.FromContext silent type assertion fallback
pluginId/endpoint depend on implicit middleware ordering
Stream methods pass through without context enrichment (pre-existing)
Test function name not updated after type rename
No test coverage for new ContextualLoggerMiddleware

false_negatives.golden_comment: The ContextualLoggerMiddleware methods (QueryData, CallResource, CheckHealth, CollectMetrics) panic when a nil request is received. This occurs because they directly access req.PluginContext (via the instrumentContext function) without first checking if req is nil. This is a regression, as previous middleware layers gracefully handled nil requests.

RuleActionsButtons is invoked with only promRule, but SilenceGrafanaRuleDrawer inside RuleActionsButtons still depends on a Grafana Ruler rule being present, so for Grafana rules coming from list views the 'Silence notifications' menu entry (now driven by Grafana Prom abilities) will toggle showSilenceDrawer without ever rendering the drawer. This means clicking 'Silence notifications' for these rules has no visible effect, even when abilities indicate silencing is allowed.

deviations.pr_title: Frontend Asset Optimization

true_positives.golden_comment: The GetWebAssets function implements an incomplete double-checked locking pattern for caching web assets. The function first checks if the cache is populated using a read lock (RLock), and if the cache is empty, it acquires a write lock to populate it. However, it fails to re-check whether the cache was populated by another goroutine while waiting to acquire the write lock.
true_positives.finding_description: Missing double-check under write lock — TOCTOU race causes redundant disk loads on cold cache

false_positives.finding_description: Ambient state access — package-level globals for cache and mutex

false_negatives.golden_comment: In addition to the missing double-check, the function has a critical flaw in its error handling: it unconditionally assigns the fetch result to the cache (line 69: entryPointAssetsCache = result) regardless of whether the fetch succeeded or failed. When an error occurs during asset fetching, result is nil, and this nil value overwrites any previously valid cache entry.

deviations.pr_title: Add Client resource type and scopes to authorization schema

true_positives.golden_comment: Inconsistent feature flag bug causing orphaned permissions. The AdminPermissions event listener, responsible for cleaning up permissions upon role, client, or group removal, is incorrectly guarded by the ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ (V1) feature flag. This is inconsistent with other methods in the class that use ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ_V2. Consequently, if ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ_V2 is enabled but V1 is not, the permission cleanup logic will not execute, leading to orphaned permission data. Cleanup should occur regardless of which fine-grained authorization version is enabled.
true_positives.finding_description: behavioral | major | Feature-flag scope creep

false_positives.finding_description: structural | minor | Dead infrastructure
behavioral | critical | Runtime crash on client deletion in V2
structural | minor | Static/instance inconsistency in test helpers
test-integrity | minor | Test isolation gap in map-roles test
structural | minor | Javadoc comment-code drift on void methods
behavioral | major | Manage-view transitivity gap at all-clients level
test-integrity | minor | Test state leak — roles not cleaned up

false_negatives.golden_comment: In hasPermission(ClientModel client, String scope), the resource lookup uses findByName(server, client.getId(), server.getId()), but AdminPermissionsSchema.getOrCreateResource creates per-client resources with the owner set to resourceServer.getClientId(), so this lookup will never find those resources and will always fall back to the 'all-clients' resource, effectively ignoring client-specific permissions.

In getClientsWithPermission(String scope), iterating resourceStore.findByType(server, AdminPermissionsSchema.CLIENTS_RESOURCE_TYPE) and returning resource.getName() will only ever consider the type-level 'Clients' resource (per-client resources have no type) and return its name, while AvailableRoleMappingResource#getRoleIdsWithPermissions expects actual client IDs to pass to realm.getClientById, which can lead to incorrect behavior or a null client and subsequent failures.

deviations.pr_title: Add Groups resource type and scopes to authorization schema

true_positives.golden_comment: In getGroupIdsWithViewPermission, hasPermission is called with groupResource.getId() and the same groupResource.getId() is added to granted, but hasPermission resolves resources by name (treating the argument as a group id) and the GroupPermissionEvaluator contract says this method returns group IDs that are later used as UserModel.GROUPS and in getUsersCount group filters. This mismatch means per-group VIEW_MEMBERS/MANAGE_MEMBERS permissions may not yield the expected group IDs for filtering and counts, and evaluation may effectively only look at the type-level 'all-groups' resource; consider revisiting whether this should operate on the underlying group ids (resource names) instead so it aligns with the JPA queries and the interface contract.

false_negatives.golden_comment: Fixed sleep in tests can be flaky; wait on condition instead

Because flusher processes are created via multiprocessing.get_context('spawn').Process, they are instances of multiprocessing.context.SpawnProcess, which on POSIX is not a subclass of multiprocessing.Process, so this isinstance check will always be false and hung processes won't be killed here.

deviations.pr_title: ref(crons): Reorganize incident creation / issue occurrence logic

true_positives.golden_comment: The function modifies the config variable to include display values but then returns the original monitor.config instead of the modified version.
true_positives.finding_description: `get_monitor_environment_context` returns original config, discarding modified copy

true_positives.golden_comment: The code fetches MonitorCheckIn objects by ID when the required data already exists in previous_checkins. This creates an unnecessary database query.
true_positives.finding_description: Redundant DB fetch when threshold=1

false_positives.finding_description: `get_failure_reason` produces broken output when `status_counts` is empty
Loop creates N occurrences for N-check-in threshold window
`monitor_environment_failed` signal fires unconditionally
Incident triggers before threshold reached with insufficient history
`get_environment()` called twice in same function
Identical `SimpleCheckIn` dict constructed at two sites

deviations.pr_title: feat(uptime): Add ability to use queues to manage parallelism

true_positives.golden_comment: The test test_thread_queue_parallel_error_handling has a docstring that doesn't match the test implementation.
true_positives.finding_description: Error handling test assertion contradicts stated intent

false_positives.finding_description: Conditional offset registration in submit error handler
Dispatch chain else-pairing fragility
Unit test bypasses +1 Kafka offset convention
Distribution test only checks coverage
Backpressure docstring claim vs unbounded queues
Bare literal duplication for default queue count

false_negatives.golden_comment: The queue.shutdown() method with 'immediate=False' parameter may not exist in the standard Python queue module. This could cause AttributeError at runtime. Verify the correct API or implement a custom shutdown mechanism.
The magic number 50 for max_wait is used repeatedly throughout the tests. Consider extracting this as a named constant to improve maintainability.

deviations.pr_title: feat(workflow_engine): Add in hook for producing occurrences from the stateful detector

true_positives.golden_comment: MetricAlertDetectorHandler inherits from StatefulDetectorHandler but only contains pass, failing to implement its required abstract methods: counter_names (property), get_dedupe_value(), get_group_key_values(), and build_occurrence_and_event_data(). This will cause a TypeError at runtime when the class is instantiated.
true_positives.finding_description: `MetricAlertDetectorHandler` uninstantiable — missing abstract method implementations

false_positives.finding_description: Multi-group test builds expected occurrence with wrong `value` (6 vs 10)
Multiple tests build expected occurrences with mismatched `value` parameter
`PriorityLevel(new_status)` relies on implicit integer alignment between independent enums
Removed duplicate-detection test with no replacement for dict uniqueness guarantee
Hardcoded identical UUIDs across all mock occurrences
group_key mismatch: occurrence built for `"val1"` but result uses `"group_key"`
`build_mock_occurrence_and_event` ignores `value` parameter — root cause of F-02/F-03

false_negatives.golden_comment: Docstring says this returns a list of DetectorEvaluationResult, but the method now returns a dict keyed by DetectorGroupKey. Consider updating the docstring to match the new return type.
